zh.Sawtry, Huntingdonshire, England: 英國亨廷格多史爾薩福崔
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret of Huntingdon於1194年出生在英國時，她的父親David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon50歲，母親Matilda of Chester23歲。她於1209年與Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway在蘇格蘭Forfarshire登迪結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1233年6月1日享年39歲。

zh-hans.Sawtry, Huntingdonshire, England: 英国亨廷格多史爾薩福崔
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret of Huntingdon于1194年出生在英国时，她的父亲David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon50岁，母亲Matilda of Chester23岁。她于1209年与Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway在苏格兰Forfarshire登迪结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1233年6月1日享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Huntingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret of Huntingdon was born in 1194, in England, her father, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, was 50 and her mother, Matilda of Chester, was 23. She married Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway in 1209, in Dundee, Forfarshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 1 June 1233, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret of Huntingdon est née en 1194, à Angleterre, son père, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Matilda of Chester, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway en 1209, à Dundee, Forfarshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 juin 1233, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret of Huntingdon 1194 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon 50 und ihre Mutter Matilda of Chester 23. Sie hat 1209 in Dundee, Forfarshire, Scotland Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Juni 1233 im Alter von 39 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret of Huntingdon è nata il nel 1194 nel England, sua padre, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, aveva 50 anni e sua madre, Matilda of Chester, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway nel 1209, nel Dundee, Forfarshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 giugno 1233, all'età di 39 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret of Huntingdonが1194年にイギリスで生まれた時，父David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdonは50歳，母Matilda of Chesterは23歳であった。1209年にスコットランドForfarshireダンディーでAlan FitzRoland Lord of Gallowayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1233年6月1日に39歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret of Huntingdon(이)가 1194년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda of Chester(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1209년 스컷랜드 Forfarshire 던디에서 Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1233년 6월 1일 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret of Huntingdon nasceu em 1194, em Inglaterra, seu pai, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Matilda of Chester, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway em 1209, em Dundee, Forfarshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de junho de 1233, com 39 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret of Huntingdon родилась в 1194 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda of Chester, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway в 1209 г. в местности дунди, Forfarshire, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 1 июня 1233 г. в возрасте 39 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret of Huntingdon nació en 1194, en Inglaterra, su papá, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Matilda of Chester, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Alan FitzRoland Lord of Galloway en 1209, en Dundee, Forfarshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de junio de 1233, a la edad de 39 años.
